Functions to track and manage the most recently launched installed app.

# Purpose
This header file, `shell_sdk.h`, provides function declarations for managing and tracking the state of installed applications within a system. It includes three functions: [`shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app`](<#shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app>), which retrieves the identifier of the most recently launched installed application; [`shell_sdk_set_last_installed_app`](<#shell_sdk_set_last_installed_app>), which sets the identifier of the last installed application; and [`shell_sdk_last_installed_app_is_watchface`](<#shell_sdk_last_installed_app_is_watchface>), which checks if the last installed application is a watchface. The file integrates with `system_app_state_machine.c` to monitor application launches, enabling the system to determine the most recently launched installed application.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `process_management/app_install_types.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### shell\_sdk\_get\_last\_installed\_app<!-- {{#callable_declaration:shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/shell/sdk/shell_sdk.h#L24>)

Returns the identifier of the most recently installed application.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the identifier of the application that was most recently installed and launched. This function is useful when you need to track or verify the last application installation event. Ensure that the application installation process has occurred before calling this function to get a valid result.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns an `AppInstallId` representing the most recently installed application.
- **See Also**: [`shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app`](<shell_sdk.c.md#shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app>)  (Implementation)


---
### shell\_sdk\_set\_last\_installed\_app<!-- {{#callable_declaration:shell_sdk_set_last_installed_app}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/shell/sdk/shell_sdk.h#L26>)

Sets the identifier of the last installed application.
- **Description**: Use this function to update the record of the most recently installed application. This function must be called whenever a new application is installed to ensure that the system correctly tracks the latest installation. It is important to provide a valid application identifier to maintain accurate state information.
- **Inputs**:
    - `app_id`: The identifier of the application that was most recently installed. It must be a valid `AppInstallId` as defined in the application installation types. The caller must ensure that this identifier is correct and valid.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`shell_sdk_set_last_installed_app`](<shell_sdk.c.md#shell_sdk_set_last_installed_app>)  (Implementation)


---
### shell\_sdk\_last\_installed\_app\_is\_watchface<!-- {{#callable_declaration:shell_sdk_last_installed_app_is_watchface}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/shell/sdk/shell_sdk.h#L28>)

Checks if the last installed app is a watchface.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine if the most recently installed application is a watchface. This function is useful when you need to verify the type of the last installed application, particularly in scenarios where the application type affects subsequent operations or user interface behavior. Ensure that the application installation process is complete before calling this function to get accurate results.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the last installed app is a watchface, otherwise returns `false`.
- **See Also**: [`shell_sdk_last_installed_app_is_watchface`](<shell_sdk.c.md#shell_sdk_last_installed_app_is_watchface>)  (Implementation)
